title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: - High: [freee-client.mjs:159](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/freee-client.mjs#L159) makes `FREEE_CLIENT_ID`/`FREEE_CLIENT_SECRET` mandatory for every call. Previously the wrapper passed them through opportunistically for token refresh; now hosts relying on already-stored tokens or non-macOS environments without Keychain will fail all bookkeeping operations before the MCP call even starts. - Medium: [eval-skill.sh:350](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L350) removed the old non-command/CJK filtering but still flags any unknown first token as a binary at [eval-skill.sh:410](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L410). Japanese explanatory lines inside ```bash``` examples will now be counted as phantom commands, creating false negatives in skill evaluation. - Medium: [experience.jsonl:96](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/experience.jsonl#L96) and the other `experience.jsonl` additions appear to commit raw session telemetry/prompts. Those entries include internal paths, hostnames, workflow details, and user task text; that is a data-leak and repo-noise risk, and these files should likely be ignored or scrubbed before commit. No additional clear logic bugs stood out in the tracked script diffs beyond those.
